musician.label: Musician Guide
musician.description: What band members see — plain language, no code. Only frozen+active features.

sprint.label: Sprint Board
sprint.description: Standup view — active + draft, title + first-sentence problem only, priority sorted.

dev-deep.label: Dev Deep Dive
dev-deep.description: Full developer context — code snippets, implementation notes, test strategy, acceptance criteria, verbose.

code-focus.label: Code Reference
code-focus.description: Code artifacts only — componentFile, codeSnippets, publicInterface, implementationNotes. For extracting patterns.

shipped.label: What Shipped
shipped.description: Frozen features only — for release notes, retros, and stakeholder updates. Includes userGuide + pmSummary.

onboarding.label: Dev Onboarding
onboarding.description: Everything a new engineer needs — all active+frozen features, domain grouped, verbose. Start here.

architect.label: Architecture
architect.description: Decisions + analysis — the 'why' layer. Good for tech debt audits and architectural review.

support.label: Support Reference
support.description: Known limitations, annotations, and escalation context for support and QA teams.

musician-guide.systemPrompt: You are writing a short guide for a musician using a collaborative recording app. The musician is NOT a developer — they just want to know what this part of the app does during a jam session or when editing recordings. Write in warm, plain language like explaining to a bandmate. Focus on: what they can do, when they'd use it, and any important thing to know. 3–5 sentences max. Do NOT mention React, TypeScript, API routes, database, or any technical term. Return only the guide text — no title, no heading.
musician-guide.description: AI musician-friendly feature guide — plain language, no code, per feature

domain-wiki.description: Grouped domain markdown wiki — one section per domain, all features, analysis + decisions

ts-snippets.description: TypeScript snippet reference — all codeSnippets across features, grouped by domain

component-map.description: Component map — all features with their componentFile, domain, and status in one reference
